ai_feedback.selected_reason: Repo-audit-specific checks were recommended, so the combined repo-audit finish gate is required.

blocking_invariants.details: Do not widen scope before that check when a narrow executable validation exists.
blocking_invariants.summary: Run the first focused executable validation immediately after the first substantive edit.

blocking_invariants.details: Targeted pytest or repo-local tool commands are the expected first validation path in this repo.
blocking_invariants.summary: Use repo venv commands, not the VS Code test runner, for first validation.

blocking_invariants.details: Python finish gates require focused behavior validation plus touched-file Ruff and Pyright checks.
blocking_invariants.summary: Do not finish Python edits with touched-file Ruff or Pyright errors.

blocking_invariants.details: Use owner-suite validation or quick repo audit after the narrow check for shared infra or cross-subsystem wiring.
blocking_invariants.summary: Shared infra and devtools changes widen only after the focused check passes.

blocking_invariants.details: Run sattlineLsp.restartServer after changes under the editor or workspace loading surfaces.
blocking_invariants.summary: Restart the language server after semantic core, LSP, editor_api, or VS Code client edits.

default_entrypoint.description: Default machine entrypoint for agents. Returns changed files, owning surface, instruction files, first focused validation, finish-gate plan, and blocking invariants in one response.

finish_gate.description: Combined repo-audit finish gate for changes that require repo-audit-specific checks.

finish_gate_template.description: Combined repo-audit finish gate for changes that require repo-audit-specific checks.

instruction_files.description: Use when changing AGENTS, custom agents, prompts, skills, hooks, or other repo AI customizations in SattLint. Covers discoverability, context efficiency, and hook safety rules.
instruction_files.name: Agent Customization Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing DOCX generation, documentation classification, FS grouping, unit scope selection, or GUI documentation workflows in SattLint. Covers docgen-specific boundaries and tests.
instruction_files.name: Documentation Generation Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing parser-core, strict validation, grammar, AST, transformer, parser fixtures, or parser tests in SattLint. Covers parser invariants and first validation routing.
instruction_files.name: Parser Analysis Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing Python tests in SattLint. Covers focused pytest routing, fixture reuse, and stable assertion patterns for repo-venv workflows.
instruction_files.name: Python Test Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing ratchet baselines, debt ledgers, approval records, coverage floors, or ratchet-policy tests in SattLint. Covers approval, monotonicity, per-file debt, and focused validation rules.
instruction_files.name: Ratchet Policy Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing repo audit checks, pipeline artifacts, public-readiness scans, portability checks, or devtools JSON outputs in SattLint. Covers preferred seams and focused validation.
instruction_files.name: Repo Audit Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when routing work to the owning SattLint surface or choosing the closest repo area for a change. Provides the condensed repo map that was removed from AGENTS.md for context efficiency.
instruction_files.name: Repo Map Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing src/ or tests/ in SattLint. Critical SattLine language invariants, validation rules, variable-analysis gotchas, CLI testing rules, and workspace/LSP boundaries that must not be broken.
instruction_files.name: SattLine Invariants

instruction_files.description: Use when changing SattLint test fixtures, corpus manifests, sample SattLine files, or golden analysis artifacts. Covers fixture shape, corpus layout, and minimal SattLine validity rules.
instruction_files.name: Test Fixture Instructions

instruction_files.description: Use when changing workspace loading, semantic core, editor facade, dirty-buffer parsing, LSP diagnostics, or VS Code client behavior in SattLint. Covers workspace-specific invariants and restart rules.
instruction_files.name: Workspace LSP Instructions

owning_agents.description: Use when: changing repo audit checks, pipeline artifacts, public-readiness scans, portability checks, secrets and PII scanning, or devtools audit commands in SattLint
owning_agents.name: Repo Audit

owning_agents.description: Use when: changing SattLine grammar, parser, transformer, AST, strict validation, analyzers, syntax-check behavior, or semantic rules in SattLint
owning_agents.name: Parser Analysis

owning_agents.description: Use when: splitting work across multiple chats or agents, parallelizing repo tasks, coordinating file ownership, planning handoffs, or orchestrating parser, LSP, and verification work in SattLint
owning_agents.name: SattLint Orchestrator

relevant_checks.ai_summary: Use after touched Python edits for fast style, import-order, and lint hygiene proof.
relevant_checks.label: Run Ruff

relevant_checks.ai_summary: Use when touched Python files need static type proof before widening to broader finish gates.
relevant_checks.label: Run pyright

relevant_checks.ai_summary: Use targeted owner pytest first for behavior proof on Python changes.
relevant_checks.label: Run pytest

relevant_checks.ai_summary: Use when routing catalogs, recommendation metadata, or generated AI registry outputs change.
relevant_checks.label: Verify recommendation metadata and routing catalog coverage

coverage.reason: Touched source files should be proven by focused changed-line coverage when executable diff lines exist, or touched-file coverage otherwise.

focused_behavior_test.reason: Code changes require at least one focused owner pytest target.

suggested_commands: python scripts/run_repo_python.py -m pytest --no-cov tests/test_pipeline_run.py tests/test_repo_audit.py tests/test_recommendation_routing.py -x -q --tb=short
mutation_guidance.suggestion: Prefer mutation-style or property-style assertions for parser, validation, and routing seams before treating the repo-wide coverage percentage as sufficient proof.

recommendation.fallback_reason: Changed files touch the repo-audit control surface, so targeted Python proof and recommendation verification are recommended instead of widening to the full supported repo-audit profile.
